page: Amazon Web Services c8g.24xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by custom 64-bit Arm-based AWS Graviton processors. It features 96 dedicated physical cores utilizing the AWS Neoverse-V2 architecture running at 2.8 GHz, with a single thread per core. The instance is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and includes a baseline network bandwidth of 40 Gbps. There is no local storage or GPU hardware bundled with this instance type.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core CPU and Geekbench multi-core scores placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Memory bandwidth is also highly efficient, reaching 480.57 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 200.76 GB/sec for write operations. The instance demonstrates strong capabilities in database operations, achieving 193.42 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks, and high-throughput static web serving at 99.4 GB/sec. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench image processing scores landing in the middle 50% tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated physical core layout and Arm64 architecture deliver high multi-threaded compute density without the overhead of hyperthreading. This makes the instance highly suitable for highly parallelized, compute-bound workloads. Optimal use cases include multi-threaded software compilation, high-throughput web serving, database operations, and medium-to-large scale LLM text generation inference where parallel CPU execution is prioritized.
description: Amazon Web Services c8g.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 96 dedicated physical cores on the 64-bit Arm Neoverse-V2 architecture. Running at 2.8 GHz with a single thread per core, it is supported by 192.0 GB of memory, a 40 Gbps baseline network, and the AWS Nitro hypervisor, with no local storage or GPU. Benchmark results show top-tier multi-core performance, high memory bandwidth, and excellent throughput for database operations and static web serving. However, single-core performance remains in the average tier. The instance offers high resource density and multi-threaded efficiency, making it well-suited for parallelized workloads such as software compilation, high-throughput web serving, database operations, and CPU-based LLM inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8g.24xlarge is an Arm64 compute-optimized instance with 96 dedicated Neoverse-V2 cores, 192 GB RAM, and 40 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.

tagline: A high-density, multi-core Arm64 compute instance delivering top-tier parallel processing and memory bandwidth for demanding non-GPU workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated physical Arm64 cores utilizing AWS Neoverse-V2 architecture at 2.8 GHz.
192.0 GB of system memory with top-tier read and write bandwidth.
40 Gbps baseline network bandwidth running on the AWS Nitro hypervisor.
Top-tier multi-core CPU, Redis, and static web serving benchmark performance.
No local storage or GPU hardware included.
Best suited for multi-threaded software compilation, database operations, and CPU-based LLM inference.
